layout: Two stacked Cards: 'Build information' and 'Runtime information'. Each contains a 2-column table (label | value).

card_title.text_side: Title in H-tag next to icon

table.row_border: Visual row-divider visible in screenshot but getComputedStyle shows border-bottom 0px none — likely applied via :not(:last-child) or row striping at Mantine Table layer; not a per-cell border.
table.note: Labels (Version, Revision, etc.) rendered as bold <th> in first column; values as <td> in second. Same table style used across Build/Runtime/Agent info cards.

Configuration_Reload.note: INCONSISTENCY: State 'Successful' is shown as plain black text. Compare to /targets where UP/DOWN states are styled as pill badges (green/red). The success state here has no visual affordance — if it said 'Failed' it would look the same. Candidate normalization: use an outlined/soft green badge 'Successful' matching the 'UP' badge style.

WAL_Corruptions.note: Numeric value rendered as plain text — fine. But consider that 0 is a 'good' value — no visual indicator.
